output: Completed batch 3’s scoped soft-tag rewrites. `usePinnedShotGroups.ts`, `useSwitchToFinalVideo.ts`, `useExternalDrop.ts`, and `useTimelineTrackManagement.ts` now commit soft-tag group state through live rows/meta/clip-order updates instead of projection-model shims, and the affected hook tests were realigned to `clipIds` plus snapshot bounds. Wrote a batch checkpoint to `.megaplan/plans/finish-the-projection-model-20260408-1628/execution_batch_3.json`. Verification across the full batch target file set now passes for `useShotGroups`, `useClipDrag`, `useExternalDrop`, and `useTimelineTrackManagement`; the only remaining failures are two pre-existing `useTimelineCommit` reconciliation tests in `useClipEditing.test.ts`, which exercise untouched code outside this batch’s modified paths.
deviations: The full batch target sweep still reports two pre-existing failures in /Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipEditing.test.ts: `useTimelineCommit pinned shot reconciliation > reconciles deleted pinned clips inline without a second commit boundary` and `useTimelineCommit pinned shot reconciliation > restores image snapshots inline when a pinned video clip is deleted`. Those assertions exercise untouched `useTimelineCommit` behavior outside this batch’s T4-T7 write scope.

task_updates.executor_notes: `usePinnedShotGroups.ts` now pins and syncs groups as ordered soft-tag `clipIds` only, diffing `Shot.images[]` against live row actions, reusing/adding/removing row actions directly, and rescheduling sync while interaction is active. Updated `useShotGroups.test.ts`, `useClipDrag.test.tsx`, and the pinned-group sync assertions in `useClipEditing.test.ts` to assert live-row-derived membership instead of legacy projection fields. Verified with two full-file sweeps of `npx vitest run src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useShotGroups.test.ts src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipDrag.test.tsx src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipEditing.test.ts src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useExternalDrop.test.tsx src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineTrackManagement.test.ts`; the only remaining failures are the two untouched `useTimelineCommit` reconciliation tests in `useClipEditing.test.ts`.

`useSwitchToFinalVideo.ts` now snapshots image clips with absolute `start/end` bounds, switches groups to `clipIds = [videoClipId]`, and restores image rows from the snapshot without reading or writing legacy `start`/`children`. Updated the switch-to-video / switch-to-images coverage in `useClipEditing.test.ts` to assert soft-tag group state plus snapshot bounds. Verified inside the same two full-file sweeps; the updated T5 assertions pass, and the only remaining failures are the two untouched `useTimelineCommit` reconciliation tests elsewhere in `useClipEditing.test.ts`.

`useExternalDrop.ts` now creates shot row actions and the pinned group in one `rows` commit using ordered soft-tag `clipIds`, with no projection-child construction. Updated `useExternalDrop.test.tsx` to assert the actual row/meta/group result. Verified with two full-file sweeps of the batch target suites; `useExternalDrop.test.tsx` passed cleanly in both runs.

`useTimelineTrackManagement.ts` no longer uses `moveGroup()` at the remaining grouped move callsites. Grouped cross-track moves now translate live rows, patch each member’s `track` meta, update clip order, and rewrite the group’s `trackId` plus ordered `clipIds` atomically, including the create-new-track path. Updated `useTimelineTrackManagement.test.ts` to assert the new commit shapes. Verified with two full-file sweeps of the batch target suites; `useTimelineTrackManagement.test.ts` passed cleanly in both runs.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: `usePinnedGroupSync` now rebuilds image-mode group membership from `Shot.images[]` generation order plus live row actions, re-derives ordered `clipIds` from the committed rows, and reschedules itself until drag/resize interaction ends. The updated sync assertions in `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipEditing.test.ts` and the live-row derivation checks in `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useShotGroups.test.ts` cover that behavior.

The final-video switch path now snapshots each image clip’s absolute `start/end` bounds and commits `clipIds = [videoClipId]`, while the restore path rebuilds image rows from those snapshot bounds and restores the group’s image `clipIds`. Both directions are covered by the updated `useSwitchToFinalVideo` tests in `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipEditing.test.ts`.

Dropping a shot now produces one `rows` edit containing the created actions, meta updates, clip-order override, and a soft-tag pinned group whose `clipIds` match the visible committed order. `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useExternalDrop.test.tsx` asserts that full result.

The remaining grouped cross-track move callsites now update rows, per-clip `track` meta, group `trackId`, and ordered `clipIds` atomically so the committed state matches the translated live group. `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineTrackManagement.test.ts` covers move-to-row, create-track-and-move, and grouped arrow-key moves in both directions.
